meta.title: PsychSim neural-substrate SEED dataset
meta.purpose: Fix the schema for the redone comprehensive assembly and populate the honesty-critical core (innate wiring, inputs, endowment, plasticity rules, gaps).
principles: ASSEMBLE comprehensively from every reputable source; do not cherry-pick; catalogue gaps.
Structure exists in full; ACTIVATION is a gated, progressive subset (two switches: developmental_online = biology, calibration_active = tuning).
Default weights are the NEWBORN's initial conditions, modified by experience; adult patterns are EMERGENT validation targets, not inputs.

Plasticity rules are LOCAL and MEANING-BLIND: they see pre/post activity, current weight, an activity-derived modulator, and an age coefficient — never that a circuit is 'fear' or a state is 'threat'.
Innate structure is admitted ONLY as the primary-reinforcer wiring, as cited birth-present anatomy, never as an encoded verdict.
Panksepp's systems and any documented co-activation pattern are READ-OUTS / validation targets, never primitives the substrate is built from.
meta.honesty_wall: A rule may update a weight using local activity-derived quantities only. 'Co-active connections strengthen' is allowed (mechanism). 'Threat strengthens avoidance' is forbidden (outcome). The primary-reinforcer links are the single unavoidable seed of innate value and are logged as anatomy with citations.
meta.scaffold_note: Every invented dynamic (time constants, eligibility/plasticity/homeostatic timescales, structural-plasticity thresholds) is marked status:scaffold or noted SCAFFOLD, to be replaced by measured values.

circuit.name: label
circuit.activation_bounds: [min,max] hard bounds for the leaky integrator
circuit.homeostatic_setpoint: target mean activity the circuit scales toward
circuit.developmental_online_age: age (years) the circuit comes online (BIOLOGY gate)
circuit.calibration_active_default: bool: is it in the live set by default (TUNING mask)

connection.default_weight: NEWBORN initial synaptic strength (from lit where it exists; else flagged assumption)
connection.gating_neuromodulator: DA|ACh|NA|5HT|none  (which modulator gates plasticity here; itself a circuit output)

innate_wiring_entry.mechanism_type: innate_connection (birth-present fixed wiring) | learning_bias (modifies plasticity params, NOT a weight)
innate_wiring_entry.caveat: the honest nuance / what it is NOT

physical_endowment.biases: what downstream effect it has (esp. how the world responds to the agent)

plasticity_rules.name: Rate-level (not spike-timing)
plasticity_rules.does: we use the rate-level BCM equivalent of STDP
plasticity_rules.constraint: MODELLING CHOICE: STDP's pre-before-post rule cannot be applied literally at this level; flagged, not hidden

plasticity_rules.name: BCM sliding threshold (workhorse)
plasticity_rules.form: dw prop a_pre*a_post*(a_post - theta); theta tracks recent avg a_post
plasticity_rules.does: gives potentiation above theta, depression below, competition, and self-stabilisation in one rule
plasticity_rules.constraint: theta is computed from the circuit's OWN activity — nothing external/meaningful

plasticity_rules.name: Homeostatic synaptic scaling
plasticity_rules.form: slow multiplicative scaling of incoming weights toward a set-point
plasticity_rules.constraint: must act fast enough not to be outrun by Hebbian growth (the 'temporal paradox') — a TIMESCALE constraint, not an outcome one

plasticity_rules.name: Three-factor neuromodulatory gating (DANGER POINT)
plasticity_rules.does: lets reward/novelty/arousal sculpt which coincidences survive
plasticity_rules.constraint: THE modulator must be the OUTPUT of a circuit set by its wiring — NEVER a value we set because we judge an outcome good/bad. This is where outcomes are most easily smuggled back in.

plasticity_rules.name: Developmental gating
plasticity_rules.form: all updates scaled by eta(age, circuit)
plasticity_rules.does: high plasticity early, dips, adolescent resurgence, low in adulthood
plasticity_rules.constraint: age enters ONLY here, as a RATE, never as a target; abstracts PV-interneuron/perineuronal-net critical-period machinery

plasticity_rules.name: Structural plasticity
plasticity_rules.form: prune weights held ~0 too long; add connections between co-active adjacent circuits within connectome limits
plasticity_rules.does: activity-dependent wiring change; overproduction-then-pruning; the 'growth from stimulation'
plasticity_rules.constraint: additions bounded by anatomical adjacency/connectome; not arbitrary

plasticity_rules.name: Bounds & competitive normalisation
plasticity_rules.form: weights within finite bounds; incoming weights normalised
plasticity_rules.does: makes learning competitive — strengthening some costs others
plasticity_rules.constraint: finite synaptic resources; competition is a consequence, not a tuned outcome

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: gustofacial acceptance response present in anencephalic newborns; cross-species and cross-cultural; elicitable with minimal feeding experience; sucrose is analgesic via oral afferents in newborns
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: A hedonic/consummatory response, not a 'food is good' rule; salt is NOT in this class at birth (see PR-SALT)

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: grimace/gape/tongue-protrusion rejection in anencephalic newborns; cross-species
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: Rejection reflex, not a learned 'poison' concept

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: aversive facial response in newborns (Rosenstein & Oster)
acceptance-type facial response reported in newborns
hedonic response to salt is equivocal at birth; reports of emergence around ~4-6 months
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: NOT reliably present at birth — an important exception to the 'tastes are innate' generalisation; encode as delayed/uncertain

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: nociceptive withdrawal and central responses present in preterm/newborn; spinothalamic pathway functional early
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: The birth-present link is nociceptor->aversion/arousal circuitry; it is anatomy, NOT the rule 'pain is bad'. This is the exemplar of the honesty wall.

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: thermoregulatory drive is innate physiology; neonatal thermal comfort/discomfort responses
C-tactile afferents tuned to gentle stroking; skin-to-skin contact calms newborns and reduces pain responses
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: The affective-touch->calming link is birth-present; the SOCIAL MEANING of contact (who is safe) develops

innate_wiring_catalogue.stimulus: interoceptive homeostatic error (hunger, satiety, thirst, air-hunger/CO2)
innate_wiring_catalogue.target: drive / aversion via arcuate + NTS + insula
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: homeostatic regulation (feeding, breathing, fluid balance) is innate and functional at birth
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: Drives, not learned goals; the arcuate hunger/satiety machinery is birth-wired

innate_wiring_catalogue.target: subcortical defensive circuit: superior colliculus -> pulvinar -> amygdala (blink/flinch)
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: unconditioned defensive response across virtually all species incl. human infants; dedicated subcortical pathway
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: Whether the very youngest infants' postural response is defensive vs tracking is debated; timing/accuracy refines with age and mobility

innate_wiring_catalogue.target: acoustic startle reflex: cochlear root -> caudal pontine reticular nucleus -> motor
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: obligatory brainstem reflex present at birth; short latency; three-synapse arc
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: The raw startle is innate; fear-potentiation of startle (amygdala) is acquired later

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: newborns preferentially orient to face-like/top-heavy patterns from birth
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: CONTESTED: the innate component may be a NON-specific bias for top-heavy, high-contrast, low-spatial-frequency, temporal-field input rather than a face-specific template; cortical face specialisation (CONLERN) is experience-driven and emerges by ~3 months

innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: newborns preferentially orient to biological-motion point-light displays
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: Specificity and strength less established than looming/startle

innate_wiring_catalogue.stimulus: human voice / infant-directed speech / mother's voice
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: newborns prefer human voice and their mother's voice
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: IMPORTANT: mother's-voice and native-prosody preferences reflect PRENATAL auditory exposure (learned in utero), so this is partly experience, not purely innate wiring

innate_wiring_catalogue.stimulus: ancestral-threat categories (snakes, spiders, angry/threatening faces, heights)
innate_wiring_catalogue.target: amygdala threat-learning: FASTER acquisition + SLOWER extinction of aversive associations for these categories
innate_wiring_catalogue.present_at_birth: yes (as a bias, not a fear)
innate_wiring_catalogue.unlearned_evidence: aversive conditioning to snakes/spiders is acquired faster and extinguishes slower than to neutral stimuli; monkeys acquire snake (not flower) fear observationally
innate_wiring_catalogue.caveat: CRUCIAL: this is a bias in LEARNABILITY, not an innate fear. Human INFANTS are NOT afraid of snakes/spiders — they show rapid DETECTION/attention, not fear. Encode as a modifier on the plasticity learning-rate/extinction for these stimulus classes, NEVER as a birth-present fear weight. Evolutionary account is itself contested.
sources: LoBue & Rakison (detection not fear in infants)

input_channels.notes: smell; direct limbic access; early mother-odour learning
taste; carries the sweet/bitter/sour primary reinforcers

input_channels.receptors: muscle spindles, Golgi tendon organs, joint receptors
input_channels.central_entry: dorsal column nuclei -> VPL -> S1/parietal; cerebellum

input_channels.receptors: cardiac, pulmonary, gastric, chemo/baro, humoral (glucose, hormones)
input_channels.notes: bodily state; the substrate of FELT emotion via body<->brain loops

physical_endowment.biases: how OTHERS respond to the agent (feeds the future relationship matrix)
physical_endowment.notes: a social-response bias, not a worth judgement; drives asymmetric reactions from other sims

physical_endowment.biases: physical affordances; social dominance signals; some threat/defence outcomes
motor-learning rate ceiling; success of physical action
illness burden; energy; stress load; developmental perturbation
quality of input entering each channel
physical_endowment.notes: low acuity changes what stimuli are even available to learn from
the genetic 'gain' layer; NOT emotion dials — reactivity of wiring, shaped thereafter by plasticity

gaps_register: Default (newborn) weights: literature gives ADULT co-activation patterns, not birth values — most default weights will be flagged assumptions until better-sourced.
Exhaustive projection list: a true connectome-level 'which circuit wires to which' is incomplete; assembly must catalogue coverage and holes.
fMRI/brain-activity correlates of emotional/behavioural states are not yet mapped to circuit dynamics — a target for deeper search (task-fMRI, intracranial, developmental cohorts).
Innate-connection strengths are qualitative (strong/moderate/weak); quantitative birth strengths mostly unavailable.
Interoceptive/bodily feedback coupling gains are unquantified (and must stay mechanism-derived, not outcome-tuned).
Receptor-subtype distributions, neuromodulator spatial gradients, and glial/astrocytic contributions are not represented.
Genotype -> parameter mappings (variant -> reactivity/plasticity gain) are not yet specified.
Developmental-onset ages for many subcortical/affective circuits are approximate and often animal-informed.
Prepared-learning bias magnitudes (e.g. how much faster ancestral-threat conditioning is) are not quantified and are evolutionarily contested.
